title: rosa × collina var. corymbifera
intro: Rosa × collina var. corymbifera, also known as the sweetbriar or coryb, is a type of shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosa × collina var. corymbifera include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating menopausal symptoms, as well as having antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, while its roots can be used to make a medicinal oil or extract.
